original_reasoning: ملخ=to tear out/madness context; μελαγχολία=melancholy. Possible semantic drift through mental disturbance
Arabic qakha = 'insides rotted from disease'; Greek = 'in bad habit of body, wasted'. Plausible semantic link via disease wasting body.
Arabic liquid-smear imagery can connect to the vessel or tool used for pouring, an action-to-instrument shift.
Kadasha (informing partly, driving) vs 'having been scattered/dispersed' - faint scatter link
Arabic ghundar = fat plump youth; Greek Gindarus = Jindires (Syrian Semitic place name). Shared Semitic substrate possible.
Arabic 'asqafa = intended good but did not do it (failed intent). Greek alaoskopie = useless/blind/careless watch (a-+laos+skope). Both encode failed/deficient vigilance/effort. Strong abstract link.
When milk changes smell it has undergone a changed state or form; Greek σχῆμα 'form, shape' preserves that resultant-state side.
Breath in the chest can naturally yield audible rattling or chatter-like sound.
